projects.name: Glas Olsson
projects.shortDescription: A Laravel project for a fictional company with a functional admin panel to manage products and categories.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A Laravel project crafted by me and my teammate. The goal was to create a functional admin panel for a fictional company where the user can manage products and their categories. From scratch we created a full CRUD system for both products and categories.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: The website is built with Laravel and uses SQlite as the database, following the MVC model for secure routes. Tailwind CSS is used for styling to create a clean and responsive design. The Chart.js library is integrated to visualize sales data and provide insights into product performance.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: We started by setting up the Laravel project and designing the database schema for products and categories. We then implemented the CRUD functionality, ensuring that users can easily create, read, update, and delete products and categories. After that, we focused on styling the admin panel with Tailwind CSS to enhance the user experience. Finally, we integrated Chart.js to display sales data in an interactive and visually appealing way. Throughout the development process, we conducted rigorous testing and debugging to ensure that all features work seamlessly and that the user interface is intuitive and responsive.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The admin panel successfully provides a user-friendly interface for managing products and categories, with all CRUD operations functioning as expected. Our rigerous testing and debugging process ensured a smooth user experience, and the integration of Chart.js allows for insightful data visualization of sales performance. This project pushed my knowlage and showed me how far I've come in just 6 months of learning web development.

projects.name: Yrgopelago
projects.shortDescription: A fullstack project for a fictional hotel company called Mos'le Comfortable.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A solo fullstack project for a fictional hotel company called Mos'le Comfortable. The project includes a complete hotel booking system where users can browse available rooms, make reservations, and manage their bookings. Additionally, users can add premium services to their bookings such as breakfast packages and spa access.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: The project is built with CSS and javascript for the frontend, while the backend is developed using PHP and SQlite database with Composer for dependency management. The application follows a structured architecture to ensure maintainability and scalability.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: The goal was to create a landing page for desktop where users can easily navigate through the hotel booking system. The development process involved designing a user-friendly interface for browsing rooms and managing reservations, implementing backend logic to handle bookings and premium services, and ensuring seamless integration between the frontend and backend components. Rigorous testing was conducted to ensure that all features function correctly and that the user experience is smooth and intuitive.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The hotel booking system provides a seamless experience for users to browse avaliable rooms and make reservations. The backend checks for room avaliability and processes bookings efficiently, while the frontend offers an intuitive interface for managing reservations and adding premium services. This project pushed my knowlage of fullstack development and provided valuable experience in creating a complete web application from scratch.

projects.name: Chirper
projects.shortDescription: A web application for interacting with a birdcall API.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: My first project working with external APIs and real-time data integration. The application allows users to interact with a birdcall API, fetching and displaying data about various birds and their calls. The goal with this project was to solve a problem and learn how to manage external API calls.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: Built with vanilla JavaScript and the Fetch API to master asynchronous data fetching and DOM manipulation.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: The project involved designing a user-friendly interface for displaying birdcall data, implementing functionality to fetch and display real-time data from the API, and ensuring that the application is responsive and accessible across different devices. I focused on optimizing the user experience by providing clear UI elements and information about the birdcalls fetched from the API.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The application successfully interacts with the birdcall API, allowing users to view data about various birds and hear their call. The project provided valuable experience in working with external APIs and handling asynchronous data in JavaScript, resulting in a functional and user-friendly web application.

projects.name: Butter-chicken
projects.shortDescription: A command-line application built with Node.js for finding optimal travel destinations based on budget and current weather.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A duo created command-line application built with Node.js for finding optimal travel destinations based on budget and current weather. The application allows users to input their weather preferences and target travel region, it then provides recommendations for travel destinations that offer the best value for money while also considering current weather conditions. The goal of this project was to create a useful tool for travelers looking to maximize their travel experience within their budget constraints.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: Built with Node.js and utilizes various NPM packages for handling user input, making API calls, and processing data. The application integrates with external APIs to fetch real-time weather data and currency exchange rates, allowing it to provide accurate and up-to-date recommendations for users.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: The development process involved identifying an existing issue and creating a solution for it. We started by designing the application's functionality and user interface, then implemented the core features using Node.js and relevant NPM packages. We integrated external APIs to fetch necessary data for providing travel recommendations, and conducted thorough testing to ensure that the application functions correctly and provides accurate recommendations based on user input.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The command-line application successfully provides users with optimal travel destination recommendations based on their region and weather preferences. The integration of real-time data from external APIs allows for accurate and relevant recommendations, making it a valuable tool for travelers looking to maximize their travel experience within their budget constraints. This project provided valuable experience in building a functional command-line application and working with external APIs in a Node.js environment.
